## ha

### Sense 1: lexicon

- **Pronunciation:** `ha`
- **Part of speech:** particle
- **Definition:** sacred or solemn speech
- **Register/status:** grammar; accepted
- **Domains:** daily-life
- **Literal:** grammatical particle: register
- **Example:** Ha na dov.
- **Corpus:** common (2370 attestations); tracks: ritual-vow; examples in ritual-001, ritual-002, ritual-011, ritual-012, ritual-013, ritual-014, ritual-015, ritual-016, ...

### Sense 2: particle

- **Pronunciation:** `ha`
- **Part of speech:** particle:register
- **Definition:** sacred or solemn speech
- **Register/status:** register; accepted
- **Domains:** daily-life
- **Literal:** Precedes a sentence or word to raise ritual seriousness.
- **Example:** Ha na dov.
- **Corpus:** common (2370 attestations); tracks: ritual-vow; examples in ritual-001, ritual-002, ritual-011, ritual-012, ritual-013, ritual-014, ritual-015, ritual-016, ...

- **Example:** Kat hamaw-ket.

- **Pronunciation:** `HASAN-GA`
- **Part of speech:** noun
- **Definition:** ongoing process, practice, or craft of: to hash content, digest data, or prove integrity by fingerprint
- **Register/status:** technical; accepted
- **Domains:** technology-software, ai-cognition, media-communication
- **Root family:** `HSN`; Technology; hashing, integrity digest; hashing, digest, integrity fingerprint, fixed proof of content
- **Pattern:** process
- **Literal:** H-S-N root, process suffix -ga
- **Example:** Hasan-ga ga ban rih.
- **Corpus:** unattested (0 attestations)

- **Pronunciation:** `HATAX-DAK`
- **Part of speech:** noun
- **Definition:** teaching, training, or disciplined study of: hat, head covering
- **Register/status:** technical; accepted
- **Domains:** body-health
- **Root family:** `HTX`; Body; hat, head covering; hat, head covering, shade, warmth, or public sign worn above
- **Pattern:** discipline
- **Literal:** H-T-X root, discipline suffix -dak
- **Example:** Tu dak hatax-dak.
- **Corpus:** unattested (0 attestations)
